behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: The failure is a malformed request (missing labels in the API call). A pure unit test of request-building logic alone cannot verify that the taxonomy list is integrated into the actual API invocation. MOCK-MISS: A mock API that accepts any request without validating structure would pass even if labels are missing. The test must inspect request arguments at the module→API boundary to catch this, requiring Integration level (mocking the HTTP client or API, then examining call arguments).
behaviors.test_description: Mock the external API client. Call the module with a document. Inspect the captured API call arguments and verify that all valid taxonomy labels are included in the request payload.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration test structure (mock API + inspect args) is appropriate for verifying request composition.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This is a local validation/sanitization behavior within the module—reading the API response and filtering invalid labels. MOCK-MISS: A mock API that returns an invalid label *would* expose the bug if validation is missing; the failure is deterministic and local. No integration with other components is necessary. A unit test that mocks the API to return an out-of-taxonomy label is sufficient to verify the module's response validation.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the external API to return a label (e.g., 'unclassified') that is not in the valid taxonomy. Call the module and assert it returns 'unknown', not the invalid label.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Unit test of response validation is appropriate; no cross-module integration needed.

behaviors.description: The classification result for the same document is the same across repeated calls.
behaviors.justification: This tests a consistency guarantee the module must provide (likely via caching, API determinism parameters, or similar). MOCK-MISS: A naive mock that always returns the same label would hide failures where the module doesn't implement caching or determinism enforcement. To properly test this, you need a mock API that returns *different* results on consecutive calls (simulating real non-determinism), then verify the module still returns the same result (proving caching or determinism is working). This requires Integration level—the module must correctly integrate a caching/determinism strategy with the mocked API. If the implementation relies on real API determinism, Agentic level may be necessary.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the external API to return different labels on consecutive calls (e.g., 'legal' on first call, 'technical' on second call). Call the module twice with the same document. Assert both calls return the same result, confirming the module's consistency guarantee is enforced.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration test (non-deterministic mock + repeated calls) correctly verifies the caching/determinism behavior.

behaviors.description: The number of tokens consumed for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: The failure is that token consumption is not tracked or enforced. The module must read token counts from API responses and enforce a budget. MOCK-MISS: A unit test of budget-enforcement logic in isolation could miss the failure if the module doesn't actually read token counts from the API response (e.g., if token fields are missing from the response handling). Integration test with mocked API responses (returning various token counts in headers or body) verifies the module correctly reads and enforces the budget.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the external API to return responses with different token counts (in response headers or body). Call the module multiple times with documents of varying lengths. Assert that each API call's token consumption is recorded and stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration test (mocked API responses + budget verification) correctly tests token tracking and enforcement.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: The failure is that rate limit errors are not handled (no retry logic). The retry behavior is local to the module (or coordinated with a retry library). MOCK-MISS: A mock API that never returns rate limit errors would hide the failure. A mock API that returns a rate limit error on the first call and success on the second call can verify retry logic is present. Integration level (mocking the API with controlled rate limit responses) is sufficient; no real network or async delays are needed in the test (these can be mocked).
behaviors.test_description: Mock the external API to return a rate limit error (HTTP 429 or equivalent) on the first call, then succeed on a retry. Call the module. Assert that the module successfully retries and returns a result, rather than propagating the rate limit error to the caller.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration test (controlled mock responses + retry verification) correctly tests retry logic without needing real network delays.
